body:
## Problem

The read path supports mixed-era/pre-M3 chunk maps with empty or short placement vectors by falling back to identity placement (`fragment index -> D-server id`). GC's safety reference set does not apply the same fallback. It only protects fragments that appear explicitly in `ChunkRef.placement`.

That means a committed pre-M3 object can still be readable, but GC can consider its fragments unreferenced. If a stale orphan record or expired pending-ledger entry points at the same fragment, GC may delete live committed data.

## Evidence

- `crates/core/src/read.rs`: `fragment_dserver()` falls back to `u64::from(index)` when `chunk.placement.get(index)` is missing.
- `crates/core/src/metadata.rs`: `ChunkRef.placement` has `#[serde(default)]`, so old records decode with an empty vector.
- `crates/custodian/src/gc.rs`: `referenced_fragments()` iterates only `chunk.placement.iter().enumerate()` and inserts no identity fallback entries for empty/short vectors.
- `crates/custodian/src/reconstruction.rs`: reconstruction already mirrors the read fallback by building placement over `0..n` and using `.unwrap_or(i as DServerId)`.

## Impact

Potential live-data loss during mixed-era operation or migration. The invariant in GC's module comment says a committed chunk-map reference must never be reclaimed, but the current reference-set computation misses references encoded by the compatibility fallback.

## Suggested fix

Centralize placement expansion for a `ChunkRef` and use it consistently in read, scrub, GC, reconstruction, and rebalance. For GC/scrub this needs scheme-aware `n` expansion:

- `EcScheme::None` -> one fragment
- `EcScheme::ReedSolomon { k, m }` -> `k + m` fragments

Add a regression where a committed inode has `placement: vec![]`, a stale orphan record references `(0, FragmentId { chunk, index: 0 })`, and GC does not delete the fragment.

milestone.title: M3 — Custodians
milestone.description: The self-maintaining durability plane (L4): GC, scrub, reconstruction, rebalance + durability telemetry. Makes single-zone trustworthy; gates the Step-2 release. Plan: proposal 0005.

title: GC must honor identity-placement fallback for pre-M3 chunk maps
